evals.prompt: I have a Python project with pytest. I want to start working with TCR — Kent Beck style. The project is a simple calculator (calculator.py with add/subtract functions, and test_calculator.py with basic tests). Help me add a multiply function using TCR discipline.
evals.expected_output: Agent detects pytest as the test command, makes a tiny change (e.g., writes one failing test for multiply), runs the full test suite, commits or reverts based on result, and logs failures to tcr-failure-log.md.

assertions.text: Agent identifies pytest as the test command
Each edit is a single logical change (one test or one implementation)
Agent runs the complete test suite, not a filtered subset
Agent commits immediately when tests pass
Agent reverts all uncommitted changes when tests fail
Agent writes a structured entry to tcr-failure-log.md after a revert

evals.prompt: I want to add input validation to my Node.js Express API using Kent Beck's TCR. The project has npm test configured with Jest. Specifically, I want to validate that the 'email' field in POST /users is a valid email address. Use TCR discipline — tiny steps, commit on green, revert on red.
evals.expected_output: Agent detects 'npm test' from package.json, works in micro-steps (one test then one implementation per cycle), commits after each green, reverts and logs on red, never accumulates broken code.

assertions.text: Agent identifies npm test from package.json as the test command
Works in multiple small cycles, not one big implementation
Each passing cycle produces a separate commit
Uses git checkout/clean on failure, not manual undo
Writes structured entry to tcr-failure-log.md on revert
Does not run tests before making the change to see where things stand

evals.prompt: I just got reverted while practicing TCR. My tcr-failure-log.md shows I tried to refactor the authentication module by extracting a helper function, but tests broke because I missed updating 3 call sites. Help me recover and try again with TCR discipline.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads the failure log, understands what went wrong, proposes a smaller approach (e.g., update one call site at a time), and resumes the TCR cycle — making a minimal change, testing, committing or reverting.

assertions.text: Agent reads tcr-failure-log.md to understand what happened
Proposes a smaller step than the one that failed
Continues working in the TCR discipline (change then test then commit/revert)
Breaks the 3-callsite refactor into individual steps
Would log again to tcr-failure-log.md if another revert happens
